english: γda (gda) how many are there here? Zam.; sgyúr-gin γda (sgyur gin gda) Glr.; rtóg-tu γdao (rtog tu gdao) he or it may be discerned, distinguished Dzl.; pýin-nas γda (phyin nas gda) he had arrived Mil.; no other negative than mi (mi) can precede it: źábs-mtil-la ču rég-pa tsám-las mi γda (zhabs mtil la chu reg pa tsam las mi gda) the water did not reach above the soles of the shoes Mil. - 2. with par (par) it expresses uncertainty, vagueness, γśégs-par γda (gshegs par gda) he may possibly go, Pth.; ˳di yín-pa (col. for par) γda ('di yin pa gda) he seems to be this (man) Pth.; cf. ˳dug-pa ('dug pa). - 3. to say, cf. mči-ba (mchi ba).
another form for ˳diń-ba ('ding ba); also sbst.: γdiń-ba dań bgo-ba (gding ba dang bgo ba) carpets and clothes, i.e. all sorts of textures, Stg.
1. another form for sdú-ba (sdu ba) to gather, to collect. 2. another form for γdúń-ba (gdung ba)(?) Sch.: to love; cf. rnyed-la γdu (rnyed la gdu) Zam.

Glr., γdú-gu (gdu gu) Glr., γdúb-bu (gdub bu) the usual form, ring for the wrist, bracelet, or for the ankle, an ornament of Hindoo women; lug (resp. pýag) - γdub (lug (resp. phyag) gdub) bracelet; rkań (resp. źabs) - γdub (rkang (resp. zhabs) gdub) foot-ring; sór (col. ser) - γdub (sor (col. ser) gdub) finger-ring Glr.; γser-γdub (gser gdub) gold-ring, dńul-γdub (dngul gdub) silver-ring; γsér-ser-γdub (gser ser gdub) a golden finger-ring; *tág-čė* (*tag che*) W. to put on (a ring).

1. poison = dug (dug), γdúg-pa γsum (gdug pa gsum) Dzl. = dug γsum (dug gsum); zás-su γdúg-pa zá-ba (zas su gdug pa za ba) Dom. - 2. in general: any thing hurtful, or any injury, mischief, harm done; as adj. noxious, mischievous, dangerous, γdúg-pa-čan (gdug pa can), of animals, demons, wicked men; dug-sbrúl γdúg-pa-čan (dug sbrul gdug pa can) dangerous venemous serpents Glr.; dre-srín γdúg-pa mań (dre srin gdug pa mang) many mischievous demons Glr.; γdúg-pai bsám-pa (gdug pai bsam pa) propensity to destroy, destructiveness, ferocity, of beasts of prey Glr.; γdúg-pai ńá-ro (gdug pai nga ro) wild screams Mil.; γdug-rtsúb (gdug rtsub) ferocity, malice, spite Mil.; stár-bu γdúg-pa tsér-ma-čan (star bu gdug pa tsher ma can) buckthorn with horrible spines Wdn.; also for mischief done by evil spirits Mil.

I. resp. dbu-γdúgs (dbu gdugs) 1. parasol, umbrella, B., C. - 2. canopy, baldachin; spyi-γdugs (spyi gdugs) a covering, shelter, awning, for several persons Glr.; γdugs ˳búbs-pa (gdugs 'bubs pa) to raise a canopy, to put up a shade or screen; of peacocks: to spread the tail. II. eleg. mid-day, noon, sáń-gi γdúgs-la (sang gi gdugs la) for to-morrow noon Dzl.; noon-tide heat (cf. dugs (dugs)), γdugs-méd γdóń-pa γdúgs-kyis γdúńs (gdugs med gdong pa gdugs kyis gdungs) an unprotected face is molested by the heat Lex.; γdugs-tsód (gdugs tshod) 1. noon-tide, dinner-time, 2. dinner.

resp. for rus (-pa) (rus (-pa)), 1. bone, bones, remains, esp. as riń-srél (ring srel), also γduń-rús (gdung rus), sku-γdúń (sku gdung); γser-γdúń (gser gdung), dńul-γdúń (dngul gdung) the gold and silver palls covering the remains of the highest Lamas. - 2. family, lineage, progeny, descendants, rigs ni rgyal-rigs-so (rigs ni rgyal rigs so), γduń-ni gau-ta mao (gdung ni gau ta mao) as to caste, he belongs to that of the ruler, as to family, he is a descendant of Gotama; also fig.: sańs-rgyás-kyi γduń (sangs rgyas kyi gdung) Dzl. the spiritual children of Buddha, the saints; γduń-brgyúd yod (gdung brgyud yod) the house, the family, is still existing Glr.; γduń (brgyud) ˳dzin-pai sras (gdung (brgyud) 'dzin pai sras) a first-born male, by whom the lineage may be continued, frq.; also for any single descendant Glr. - γduń-sgróm (gdung sgrom) Sch. coffin, Schr. funeral urn. - γduń-rtén (gdung rten) funeral pyramid containing relics, cf. mčod-rten (mchod rten). - γduń-rabs (gdung rabs) generation, ńá-nas γduń-rábs lńá-pa-la (nga nas gdung rabs lnga pa la) in the fifth degree after me Glr.

, γdúńs-pa (gdungs pa) I. vb. 1. to desire, to long for, zás-la (zas la), ltó-la (lto la), Glr. and elsewh.; *duń duń čó-če* (*dung dung cho che*) W. id. - 2. to love, śiń-tu γdúń-bai ma γčig (shin tu gdung bai ma gcig) my own dearly beloved mother! cf. brtse-γdúń (brtse gdung). - 3. to feel pain, to be pained, tormented, afflicted, by heat or cold, thirst, lust, distress; *nyiń duń-te* (*nying dung te*) W. sad, sorrowful; γdúń-bar byéd-pa (gdung bar byed pa) to make sad, to distress, γźán-gyi séms-la (gzhan gyi sems la), the mind of others. - 4. to be dried, nyi-mas (nyi mas) by the sun, of a dead body Dzl. II. sbst. 1. desire, longing, lust, γdúń-ba źi (gdung ba zhi) (sensual) desire ceases Sty. - 2. love, mos-gus-γdúń-ba dpag-méd skye (mos gus gdung ba dpag med skye) immense veneration and love arises Glr. - 3. affliction, misery, distress, torment, pang, γdúń-bai skad (gdung bai skad) a plaintive voice, doleful cry Glr.
